runtime_thought.image-hint: harsh stage light catching the edge of something cheap and exposed, eyes forced to register it
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The seam remains visible and undeniable; the attempt to rationalize it away only deepens the ache of seeing through.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He knows the seam is real—the foam-core edge, the fingerprint pressed into clay—and the knowing won't dissolve no matter how many times he walks this corridor. The rationalization forms anyway, a thin story laid over the thing he can't unsee and can't name.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low frequency drone with small sharp clicks—the sound of something artificial being noticed

s1_seeing_through.description: Snake notices the seams. The foam-core edge. The fingerprint in the clay. He can't unsee it but he can't say it.

s2_the_mission.description: The mission is urgent. The timer is real. The explosives are real. But the mission is happening inside a model.

s3_the_edge.description: The wall is also the edge of the set. Beyond it there is no geography, only backstage. Escape is stepping into nothing.

s4_the_ring.description: The wrestling ring. The only honest place. Here, performance is the stated purpose. Everywhere else it's a secret.

runtime_thought.image-hint: corridor repeating, one detail suddenly sharp and unavoidable in the stage light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The seam has stopped being invisible; clarity persists even when it costs him, and the wall no longer holds its secret.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He walks the corridor again, same wall, same light catching the seam—and this time the rationalization doesn't even pretend to work. The fingerprint in the clay isn't a flaw in the craft; it's proof something real was here, and he can't unknow that.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: footsteps echoing with a single high frequency that won't fade

s1_seeing_through.description: Snake notices the seams. The foam-core edge. The fingerprint in the clay. He can't unsee it but he can't say it.

s4_the_ring.description: The wrestling ring. The only honest place. Here, performance is the stated purpose. Everywhere else it's a secret.

runtime_thought.image-hint: close-up of a thumb tracing a foam edge under harsh overhead light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The knowledge of the seam is fully awake in him now, and the silence around it is no longer passive—it is a choice he keeps making.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He runs his thumb along the edge again in his mind—foam, not stone, the weight all wrong—and for a second imagines saying it out loud, just naming it, watching the whole set collapse into itself. But the checkpoint is real enough, the people are real enough, and the words stay behind his teeth where they've always lived.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a single dry click, then silence pressing back in

s1_seeing_through.description: Snake notices the seams. The foam-core edge. The fingerprint in the clay. He can't unsee it but he can't say it.

runtime_thought.image-hint: close on a thumb-shaped dent in wet clay under a single harsh light
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He has chosen silence again, but the choice is getting heavier each cycle, and the reversal—saying it aloud—is no longer unthinkable.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The fingerprint in the clay is his—he knows the pressure of his own thumb—and the stage light catches the foam edge at exactly the angle that makes denial impossible, so he keeps his face neutral and lets the silence do the lying for him. It isn't cowardice, he tells himself; it's patience, it's mission discipline, it's the long game. But the reversal is already forming: what if saying nothing is the most explosive thing he could do.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low sustained drone with a faint creak of pressure, nothing resolving

runtime_thought.image-hint: figure frozen mid-motion between a ticking device and a mirror showing his own watching face
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He now carries the active knowledge that the mission's realness and its unrealness are both true at once, and silence has become a performance he can no longer quite believe in.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The countdown is real—he can feel it in the urgency pressing against his ribs—but the mission itself lives in a model, a rehearsal space where saying nothing is still a choice he can defend. The seam between what's actually happening and what he's performing is getting harder to unsee.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: digital clock pulse layered under shallow breathing, a slight metallic tremor in the air

runtime_thought.image-hint: fluorescent light catching the raw edge of painted cardboard, eyes that don't move toward it
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The seam is seen and unsayable, and the act of not-saying it has become indistinguishable from the performance itself.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The fingerprint is still there in the clay—he keeps not-looking at it, which is its own kind of looking, and the checkpoint light hits the foam-core edge the same way it hits a real wall except it doesn't. He can feel the grimace forming somewhere behind his face, held back by the same discipline that holds everything back, and he wonders if the discipline is the mission or if the mission is just the discipline's excuse.

runtime_thought.image-hint: figure frozen mid-stride on empty street, hand raised toward invisible wire
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The boundary between practicing the mission and living it has dissolved; urgency now feeds on its own realness regardless of substrate.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The timer's tick is real even if the explosives exist only in the model's logic—and that distinction collapses the moment she has to *act* as though they're real. The rehearsal has become the mission; the performance is the sincerity.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: digital clock tick layered with breath-hold, no distinction between the two

runtime_thought.image-hint: a man pausing mid-stride in a corridor, eyes fixed on a seam in the wall where the paint doesn't quite meet
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The fingerprint in the clay stays visible behind every next step; seeing through the stage doesn't stop him from walking it.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The corridor repeats itself—same scuff on the baseboard, same angle of light that doesn't come from anywhere real—and Snake knows the set has been walked before, that someone built this and left their thumb in the wet clay. He can't call it out without unraveling the whole thing, including himself inside it.

runtime_thought.image-hint: silhouette at the boundary between stage light and absolute darkness, one foot already in the non-geography
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The edge pulls him forward not as escape but as the only honest place left—where the set ends and what he actually is becomes visible.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: The wall ahead isn't a boundary—it's a threshold he's already crossed in imagination a hundred times, and his body knows the difference between the stage light he can see and the actual nothing waiting beyond it. The fingerprint in the clay moves with him, proof that he was here, that the performance is real even when the geography ends.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: distant headlights cutting through silence, then nothing—the sound of air with no walls to bounce off

runtime_thought.image-hint: a thumb-ridge in clay wall caught under a single overhead beam
runtime_thought.residue-summary: He holds the knowledge of the seam like a splinter—too small to pull out, too sharp to ignore—standing at the edge where the light stops pretending.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He stands at the checkpoint and the overhead light catches the seam where the wall doesn't quite meet the floor—foam-core, he thinks, someone pressed a thumb into it and left a ridge—and the whole place shudders slightly in his vision, not collapsing but thinning. He can't say it out loud because saying it would make him the one who broke it, and he's not sure anymore which side of the set he's standing on.

runtime_thought.image-hint: thumb resting on a seam in painted plaster, stage light catching the ridge
runtime_thought.residue-summary: The fingerprint in the clay is now a fact he carries in his body rather than his mouth, and silence has become its own kind of complicity.
runtime_thought.thought-beat-text: He keeps running his thumb along the edge where the clay doesn't quite meet the plaster, the way you tongue a loose tooth—not to fix it, but because you can't stop proving it's real. If he names it aloud the whole checkpoint becomes a stage, and then he's standing in the wings of something he was supposed to believe in. So he says nothing, and the fingerprint stays there, accusing the wall of being a wall.
runtime_thought.audio-hint: low ambient hum with a single dry creak, then held silence
